rulings.Case Title: KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL VS. 640 OCTAVIA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Successful Opposition To Defendant Justin Huttos Frivolous Anti-Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF KALLE PIEPER, JOSE MONTOYA Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Successful Opposition To Defendant Justin Huttos Frivolous Anti-Slapp Motion is GRANTED as requested. No opposition filed by the twice continued deadline. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAEED FAZELL VS. JULIE A MARGETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Notice Of Moiton Of Petition For Instructions To 1 Cancel Sales Contract 2 To Retain Non Refundable Deposit And 3 For Permission To Seek Recovery Of Any Loss That Might Be Occasioned By A Resale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2022 line 2. OTHER MICHAEL KASOLAS Re Notice Of Moiton Of Petition For Instructions To 1 Cancel Sales Contract 2 To Retain Non Refundable Deposit And 3 For Permission To Seek Recovery Of Any Loss That Might Be Occasioned By A Resale is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. Service by mail on June 27, 2022 allows only 2 days for mailing; POS is not sufficient for e-service as to the buyers. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY CHI HUA VS. DAMES SHOUMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Tony Huas First, Fourth, Fifth, Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, Ninth, And Tenth Causes Of Action Against Dames Shouman
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT DAMES SHOUMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Tony Huas First, Fourth, Fifth, Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, Ninth, And Tenth Causes Of Action Against Dames Shouman is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to show the existence of a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. All objections to Hua Declaration are sustained except objection #2, 3, and 16. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CONSOLIDATED PROPERTY MANAGEMENT VS. BIANCA IVANIA SLAVIERO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT LILIANA BOUVET DEMURRER to COMPLAINT This is a two-part ruling. Part One: Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is transferred to department 305 to be heard by the Honorable Harry Jacobs per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Part Two: Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is continued to July 27, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. for Plaintiff to file a supplemental opposition not to exceed 2 pages addressing the argument raised in reply 4:23-5:23. Supplemental brief to be filed and served no later than July 25, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. =(305/HJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. CHARLES MULIERI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing Of Non Party Mission Housing Development Corporation To Quash And/Or Modify Defendants Subpoena(W/First Appearance Fee)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 22, 2022 line 7. OTHER MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION Motion And Notice Of Hearing Of Non Party Mission Housing Development Corporation To Quash And/Or Modify Defendants Subpoena(W/First Appearance Fee) Hearing Required. Parties to the motion to appear at 8:30 a.m. on July 22, 2022 and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts to narrow the requests to encompass only information leading to admissible evidence relevant to defendant's tenancy and defendant's eviction, production of which will not infringe on the privacy rights of third parties, financial information of the parties and the deponent and not be overly burdensome. The parties are encouraged to start their meet and confer efforts as soon as possible and provide a copy of their suggested/agreed upon modification to the request to the Court at 9:30 a.m. on July 22, 2022. This submission may be made electronically. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
